item.title: That's not how women work....

Stolen from r/ThatsNotHowWomenWork via BoredPanda
An oldie but a goodie.
This one makes me so angry. So f'ing mad.
Shocking how many of these are "I don't think women are people."

item.dc:creator: jeandolly

item.title: MAKE SWORDS GREAT AGAIN ⚔️ Join me in my fight for sword holding normalization!!!
item.description: can't a girl just like to pose with a sword nowadays damn ⚔️ I am not being really serious in this video by the way, I thought it was really funny that holding swords would polarize opinions so much hahaha OH but I was beung serious about putting a sword in as many character's hands as I can even if it isn't canon Edit : Yes the video is slightly sped up. Why? Well, I posted it at normal speed on other platforms (you can watch it at NORMAL SPEED HERE : https://www.instagram.com/reel/C51n727Sot1/ ) but Imgur limit is only 60seconds per video only and I tried my best to reduce the length but I HAD TOO MUCH TO SAY ? about swords I guess that I couldn't see which part I could cut further, so I increased speed to have the video go from 90 seconds to 60 seconds. Mystery solved hehe!!
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: Salma Hayek Says Her Family Won't Let Her Take Swimsuit Photos in 'Peace'
item.description:
(I did not make this video).
Salma Hayek can't get a moment of peace as she tries to take some bikini photos. On Monday, April 15, the actress, 57, shared several photos to Instagram of her attempts to pose in her swimsuit as she gets out of a pool — only to be foiled by her family spraying water from a hose in her face as she climbs up the ladder. "When your family won’t let you take a bikini ріс in peace."
And a throwback ...
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: 60% of the time, it works every time
item.dc:creator: TheJokerXX

item.title: I'm tired, boss.
item.description: Normally one looks forward to hearing about an MRI coming back with negative results on anything serious, but I was honestly banking on the hope that there was. The doctor said that at this point, it'd be in my best interest to pursue a path to help identify the cause of this pain, suggesting that it might be some sort of hereditary thing, but I've never known any family, living or non, that had to deal with this sort of issue. She said it could be something like arthritis or another disability, too. 90% of my work experience is in retail (stocking) or physical labor jobs, so I have minimal relevant experience in career paths that I COULD strive for with the potential of this. It certainly doesn't help that money's already tight. All I know is that I'm so very tired. In any case, thanks for tuning in to another installment of my whining. And if you haven't heard it yet today, or don't hear it enough: I love you and am proud of all you've done. As always, Shine Brightly, Beautiful People. Viral edit: Thank you to everyone who offered support, either through kind words or by helping me realize that this doctor in all likelihood sucks. Thankfully, she was my Workman's Comp doctor and not my Primary- who, by the way, has been great towards me. Hopefully he can help me explore more avenues as he has readily done so for other issues of mine in the past. And while I'm not looking forward to the inevitable medical costs for this, I had already planned on filling medical bankruptcy at some point in my life due to upcoming transition costs. I'm admittedly feeling a bit overwhelmed by all these potential solutions, so I'll have to sleep on it and hopefully discuss it with my primary soon. Thank you for all of your help~♡
item.dc:creator: MatronOfMorgues

item.title: Yayyyyyy!!!!!
item.description: After all the horror stories I've been told and read that you always get turned away if you're not old enough or you don't have kids, I was told yes! No babies for meeeeee! God, it's such a relief! Note: I used the phrase getting my tubes tied since it's colloquial, but they're actually just removing the tubes entirely. Weeeee!
item.dc:creator: Thisisallyourfault
